title: Port Said Lighthouse
description: The first lighthouse in the world to be built from reinforced concrete. An iconic octagonal tower standing 56 meters high, symbolizing the maritime history of the Suez Canal.

title: Suez Canal Authority Building (The Dome)
description: A magnificent architectural landmark originally built as the administrative headquarters for the Suez Canal Company. It features three green domes and combines Islamic and European architectural elements.

title: De Lesseps Statue Base
description: The monumental pedestal where the statue of Ferdinand de Lesseps once stood. It remains a historical landmark at the northern entrance of the Suez Canal, offering panoramic views of incoming ships.

title: Al-Abbasi Mosque
description: Built by Khedive Abbas Helmy II, this is the second oldest mosque in Port Said and remains a prime example of Islamic architecture from the Khedivial period, featuring elaborate decorations.

title: Al-Tawfiqui Mosque
description: The very first mosque established in Port Said. It holds significant historical value as the city's oldest Islamic place of worship, predating the Abbasi mosque.

title: St. Eugénie Church
description: The oldest church in Port Said, built in a Neo-Baroque style. It stands on the site of an earlier wooden chapel and is named after the Empress Eugénie of France.

title: Latin Cathedral (Cathedral of Our Lady)
description: A massive cathedral featuring imposing architecture, serving the Catholic community and standing as a testament to the city's cosmopolitan past.

title: Coptic Orthodox Cathedral of the Virgin Mary
description: A prominent Coptic cathedral known for its beautiful icons and religious significance to the local Christian community.

title: Simon Arzt Building
description: A historic department store building with a distinctive facade, once one of the most famous shopping destinations for travelers transiting the Suez Canal.

title: Italian Consulate Building
description: An architectural gem reflecting Italian Fascist-era architecture, originally serving as the consulate for the large Italian community in the city.

title: Obelisk of the Martyrs
description: A Pharaonic-style obelisk erected as a memorial to the martyrs of Port Said who died defending the city during the Tripartite Aggression in 1956.

title: Port Said Military Museum
description: A museum documenting the military history of the city, particularly the 1956 Suez Crisis, featuring outdoor displays of captured tanks and aircraft.

title: Port Fuad Historic Villas
description: A district characterized by French-style villas and gardens, designed by the Suez Canal Company for its employees, preserving a unique European ambiance.

title: Tell Tinnis (Tennis Island)
description: The ruins of a wealthy medieval island city famous for its textiles. It was abandoned and destroyed during the Crusades. A major hidden archaeological gem.

title: Tell El-Gamil
description: Site of an old tower or fortification guarding the coastal entrance to Lake Manzala.

title: Tell El-Loulou
description: Archaeological mound located on an island in the lake, containing settlement remains.

title: Tell El-Qanater
description: Archaeological site with remains of ancient hydraulic structures or settlements.

title: Tabiet El-Gamil (Fort)
description: Historic airfield and fortification area that saw heavy fighting during the 1956 war.

title: Commonwealth War Cemetery
description: Beautifully maintained cemetery containing the graves of Commonwealth soldiers who died in the world wars.

title: Villa Fernande
description: One of the oldest surviving wooden structures in the city, offering a glimpse into early canal-era architecture.

title: Greek Orthodox Church
description: Historic church serving the Greek community, featuring distinctive Byzantine-style architecture.

title: Bishara Building
description: A notable heritage building with distinct architectural features in the old Quarter.

title: Tell Ibn Salam
description: Archaeological site containing the shrine and mosque of Sheikh Ibn Salam, a medieval ascetic.

title: Tell El-Farama (Pelusium) - Port Said Access
description: The site of Ancient Pelusium. Although geographically mostly in Sinai, it is often managed/accessed via the East Port Said zone.

title: Grand Hotel de la Poste
description: One of the historic hotels of the city, reflecting the golden age of tourism.

title: House of the Canal Administration (Heia)
description: The surrounding complex of the main Dome building, including gardens and ancillary historic structures.
